data.title: Samsung, SK hynix rise as K-Chips Act clears key legislative hurdle
data.description: South Korean semiconductor stocks surged on Wednesday morning as expectations grew for the passage of the K-Chips Act, which would increase tax deductions for facility investments in the industry. Samsung Electronics Co. shares were trading 2.11 percent higher at 58,100 won ($40.3) as of 10:38 a.m. The shares have gained in six of the last eight trading sessions. SK hynix Inc. was up 3.57 percent at 217,500 won as of 10:39 a.m., briefly hitting 218,500 won with a 4.05 percent gain earlier in the session. Hanmi Semiconductor Co. shares soared 9.76 percent to 108,000 won, while other semiconductor-related stocks also saw strong gains, including PSK Holdings (4.67 percent) and Hana Micron (6 percent). The rally follows a Tuesday decision by the National Assembly’s Strategy and Finance Committee, which approved amendments to the Restriction of Special Taxation Act, known as the K-Chips Act. If the bill passes the National Assembly’s plenary session, the tax credit rate for facility investments in the semiconductor sector will increase from 15 percent to 20 percent for large and mid-sized firms and from 25 percent to 30 percent for small businesses. Meanwhile, LS Group stocks surged after xAI, Elon Musk’s AI company, unveiled its new AI model Grok 3. LS Networks stock was trading 14.37 percent higher at 4,975 won as of 10:42 a.m., having jumped as much as 24.60 percent earlier in the session. Other LS affiliates also gained, with LS Eco Energy up 2.41 percent and LS Electric rising 1.41 percent. Investors are betting on LS Electric’s potential role in supplying data center infrastructure for xAI, alongside rising expectations for increased power demand in the United States. Musk introduced Grok 3 as “the smartest AI on the earth, excelling in math, science, and coding.”

data.title: Retail Food Group delivers $66.5m revenue in H1 FY25, secures Firehouse Subs deal
data.description: Multi-brand food franchise manager Retail Food Group has delivered $66.5 million in underlying revenue and $16m in underlying EBITDA for the first six months of the 2025 financial year. The Queensland-based group reported 24.7% revenue growth on the previous corresponding period (pcp) including a full six-month contribution of $10.2m from Beefy’s Pies, acquired in late 2023 for $10m. Underlying EBITDA was up 4.2% on the pcp and 15.8% on the second half of the 2024 financial year. Network sales Sales from the group’s domestic network grew 3.2% on the previous six-month period as core coffee, café and bakery brands Gloria Jean’s, Donut King, Brumby’s and Beefy’s traded through challenging economic conditions. The Beefy’s acquisition partially offset lower network sales in the group’s quick-service restaurant (QSR) segment. Chief executive officer Matt Marshall was pleased with the group’s half-year performance. “This is an excellent set of interim results [and] we continue to invest in the quality of our retail systems to enhance our network whilst targeting short-, medium- and long-term growth opportunities,” he said. New outlets Retail Food Group opened 25 new outlets across its core brands during the period, while the closure of 17 low-performing outlets is expected to improve overall network quality in the future. Average weekly sales of the new outlets were 35% stronger than those of the outlets that closed. The company completed its acquisition of CIBO Espresso in December and expects CIBO to contribute an additional 22 outlets in the second half of 2025. Cash reserves Retail Food Group had cash reserves of $21.4m at the end of December including unrestricted cash on hand of $17.7m, resulting in net debt of $7.1m. Underlying operating cash conversion was 61% for the period, with the first-half timing of payments for 2024 short-term incentive plans and annual expenses, including insurance premiums, having an impact. Retail Food Group’s debt facility at the end of December included $15m in undrawn facilities. Firehouse Subs agreement The company has also signed a 20-year development agreement with Canadian company Restaurant Brands International (RBI) to launch the Firehouse Subs brand in Australia. Founded in 1994, the sandwich chain has more than 1,300 outlets across North America and expanded overseas after RBI acquired it in late 2021. Mr Marshall said the company plans to open 165 new Firehouse Subs outlets across Australia over the next decade, with the first to launch in south-east Queensland later this year. Perfect opportunity “We have rejected other international brands in search of the perfect opportunity which aligns with our core competencies and we have spent over 12 months completing due diligence, including multiple market visits to assess the suitability of Firehouse Subs for Australia,” he said. “Firehouse Subs is a key pillar of our ambitious growth strategy and we are confident consumers will be excited by the brand’s high-quality sandwich product together with an excellent guest experience.” RBI is one of the world’s largest QSR companies, with over $40 billion in annual sales and more than 30,000 restaurants in over 120 countries. Mr Marshall said the Australian sandwich sector – which is estimated to be worth over $1.7b and continues to grow, with only one national chain competitor – presented an attractive proposition in comparison to other food categories.

data.title: GRUPO COMERCIAL CHEDRAUI, S.A.B. DE C.V. FOURTH QUARTER 2024 RESULTS
data.description:
GRUPO COMERCIAL CHEDRAUI, S.A.B. DE C.V. FOURTH QUARTER 2024 RESULTS

PR Newswire

MEXICO CITY, Feb. 18, 2025

MEXICO CITY, Feb. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Grupo Comercial Chedraui, S.A.B. de C.V. reports its 2024 fourth quarter results. All figures are shown in nominal terms and reported under International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS).

4Q'24 Highlights:

  • Same Store Sales (SSS) growth of 2.9% in Mexico exceeded ANTAD for the eighteenth consecutive quarter.
  • SSS Growth at Chedraui USA of 0.8% in U.S. dollars.
  • The consolidated EBITDA Margin was 7.6%, excluding the impact of RCDC, the margin was 8.0%.
    -- EBITDA Margin for Retail and Real Estate Mexico was 8.6%

    -- EBITDA Margin for Chedraui USA was 6.8%, excluding RCDC impact it
    was 7.4%.
  • Net debt to EBITDA ratio was -0.18x at the end of the fourth quarter.
  • CAPEX amounted to 4.1% of sales, due to higher organic growth and RCDC investments.
  • Continued growth with the opening in 4Q'24 of 57 stores in Mexico and 2 in the U.S.

Antonio Chedraui, Grupo Comercial Chedraui's CEO, remarked:

In 2024, we accelerated our organic growth by opening 84 stores in Mexico and 6 in the United States, bringing the total number of stores to 541 and 384 respectively.

Our customer-centric strategy continued to deliver positive results, which has led us to continue to gain market share against our competitors. In Mexico, same-store sales (SSS) continue to exceed ANTAD's levels, with an average differential of 168 bps throughout fiscal year 2024*.

Our loyalty program, "Mi Chedraui," remains a key component in enhancing our relationship with customers and understanding their preferences. By the end of the year, we had built a detailed customer database of 13 million members, marking a 5.7% increase compared to 2023. Furthermore, this program enabled us to uniquely identify 74% of sales in Mexico, allowing us to leverage advanced analytics for our promotional campaigns.

At Chedraui USA, our customer base continues to grow, primarily due to a mid-single-digit traffic increase at El Super and Fiesta Mart and a marginal increase at Smart & Final. It should be noted that the campaigns implemented at Smart & Final in the second half of the year aimed to increase customer traffic and sales.

CAPEX for fiscal year 2024 was $11,454 million, representing 4.1% of consolidated sales, and is 53.0% higher than in 2023, explained by the acceleration in organic growth in Mexico and the United States and the investment made in the Rancho Cucamonga distribution center in the United States, which will allow us to obtain improvements in the EBITDA margin of El Super and S&F of around 50 basis points by 2026.

It is important to note that the cash generated in the operations of both countries allowed us to achieve a net cash position at the end of 2024 of $4,330 million pesos, equivalent to a 0.18x net cash to EBITDA ratio.

We are pleased to announce that due to our strong cash flow generation in 2024, the Board of Directors has agreed to recommend a distribution plan for our investors. This plan includes an ordinary dividend of 1,007 million pesos to be distributed in April of this year, along with an extraordinary dividend, which will be paid in two installments of 570 million pesos each in November and December. In total, the dividends for the year will amount to 2,147 million pesos, representing 32.0% of the net income attributable to the controlling interest in 2024.

*excluding the effect of the sales of groceries to support the emergency of Acapulco Guerrero registered in 2023.

To access the full document, please click here.

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Falls After RBNZ Sends Dovish Signal — Market Talk
data.description: The New Zealand dollar falls against other major counterparts in the morning Asian session after RBNZ sends a dovish signal on monetary policy. New Zealand's central bank said this morning that it has room to lower rates further this year, moving the official cash rate to a level that is much less restrictive. The market has taken the RBNZ's rate guidance as a dovish signal, RBC Capital Markets' Alvin T. Tan says in an email. "NZD has been hit as a result, and the AUD/NZD cross rate has bounced to the top of the 5-month range around 1.1180," the head of Asia FX Strategy says. NZD/USD falls 0.4% to 0.5680; AUD/NZD rises 0.3% to 1.1166. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Finder Energy Holdings Says Modelling Predicts High Oil Production at Oil Fields Offshore Timor-Leste
data.description:
Finder Energy Holdings said dynamic reservoir modeling predicts an initial production rate in the range of 25,000 to 40,000 barrels of oil per day, depending on facility constraints, at its Kuda Tasi and Jahal oil fields offshore Timor-Leste, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The model forecasts that 10 million barrels of oil would be produced in the first 18 months of production.

The modeling was conducted by Finder and Tyke Petroleum Consultants.

Flow testing of the Kuda Tasi-2 appraisal well demonstrated the potential for flow rates of over 20,000 barrels of oil per day per production well.

The firm started discussions with oil traders in Asia, noting the light sweet crude from oil fields would be sold to refineries in the region.

data.title: South Korean Stocks Rally for 7th Day
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI climbed 1.5% to around 2,666 points on Wednesday, rallying for the seventh consecutive session to reach an almost four-month high.

The rise was primarily driven by gains in technology stocks, despite ongoing concerns over potential US tariffs expected to be announced early next month.

Among index heavyweights, chip giant Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix jumped 2.1% and 3.3%, respectively.

In the battery sector, LG Energy Solutions (4.3%) and Samsung SDI (3%) led the advance.

Notable gains were also seen among automakers despite Trump’s 25% tariff threat set to be announced as early as April 2, with Hyundai Motor rising 1.3% and Kia Corp adding 1%.

Investors now look ahead to a series of key economic releases later this week, including consumer and business confidence figures, producer prices, and the Bank of Korea's monetary policy decision next week.

data.title: Hong Kong Shares Fall, Weighed by Consumer, Tech Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index falls 1.0% to 22740.15, weighed by consumer and tech stocks. Markets are likely digesting Trump's remarks of possible 25% tariffs or more on autos, chips and pharmaceutical products. Chinese home prices which narrowed their decline in January are also in focus. Decliners include JD.com, which is down 3.8%, Meituan off 2.8% and Baidu, which is 2.6% lower. The Hang Seng Tech Index is down 1.5%. Among the gainers, China Mengniu Dairy is up 8.8%, SMIC gains 2.1% and China Hongqiao Group is 2.0% higher. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Harmonic Drive Systems' Losses Narrow in Fiscal Nine Months; Shares Jump 9%
data.description:
Harmonic Drive Systems reports net loss of 354 million yen for the nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, narrowing from the net loss of 402 million yen a year ago.

The manufacturer of precision servo actuators' loss per share was 3.73 yen compared with 4.23 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo Stock Exchange filing on Feb. 7.

Sales revenue declined 3.7% to 40.36 billion yen from 41.93 billion yen a year earlier.

Harmonic Drive Systems forecasted an attributable profit of 3.6 billion yen or 37.90 yen per share for the fiscal year ending March 2025, with net sales expected to fall 3.2% to 54 billion yen.

The company maintained its forecast for an annual dividend of 20 yen per share, comprising an interim and year-end dividend of 10 yen each.

Harmonic Drive Systems' shares gained nearly 9% in late-morning trade Wednesday.

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Is Investigating Synchrony Financial (SYF) And Encourages Stockholders to Connect
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 18, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Synchrony Financial ("Synchrony" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Synchrony securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/SYF.

Investigation Details

On January 28, 2025, Synchrony announced its financial results for Q4 2024 and revealed that it missed its earnings for the quarter, pointing to weaker-than-expected net interest income. Additionally, the Company's provision for credit card losses was higher than estimates, and its net charge-off rate rose as well. Following this news, Synchrony stock dropped.

What's Next?

If you are aware of any facts relating to this investigation or purchased Synchrony securities, you can assist this investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/SYF. You can also contact Peretz Bronstein or his client relations manager, Nathan Miller, of Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC: 332-239-2660

There is No Cost to You

We represent investors in class actions on a contingency fee basis. That means we will ask the court to reimburse us for out-of-pocket expenses and attorneys' fees, usually a percentage of the total recovery, only if we are successful.

Why Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is a nationally recognized firm that represents investors in securities fraud class actions and shareholder derivative suits. Our firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors nationwide.

data.title: Thai baht/US dollar slightly weaker on Wednesday
data.description:
The Thai baht weakened slightly against the U.S. dollar on Wednesday.

At 0146 GMT, the baht was 0.06% lower at 33.66 per dollar, after trading in a range of 33.650 to 33.675. It ended the previous session at 33.64 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

data.title: Shareholders that lost money on Venture Global, Inc.(VG) should contact Levi & Korsinsky about pending Class Action - VG
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 18, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Venture Global, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/venture-global-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=130427&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: This lawsuit is on behalf of all shareholders that purchased stock pursuant and/or traceable to Venture's registration statement for the initial public offering held on or about January 24, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the complaint, Venture completed its initial public offering on January 27, 2025, selling 70 million shares at $24.00 per share. On February 5, 2025, TotalEnergies, an energy company that was a target customer of Venture, rejected opportunities to become a long-term customer of Venture, citing lack of trust. In particular, TotalEnergies CEO, stated that he was approached by Venture to see if the company would be interested in a long-term supply contract for liquefied natural gas from the Calcasieu Pass terminal in Louisiana, but he rejected the offer "because of what they are doing."

Venture is currently facing legal challenges from existing large clients, such as BP and Shell, due to delays in supply contracts as Venture commissions its projects. Given the fact that defendants ability to deliver liquefied natural gas (LNG) to the world and to continue development of Venture's five natural gas liquefication and export projects depends on customer contracts, defendants' failure to account for and address these issues caused statements in Venture's registration statement to be false and/or materially misleading at the time of the initial public offering.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Venture stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/venture-global-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=130427&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

data.title: Shareholders that lost money on Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc.(REGN) should contact Levi & Korsinsky about pending Class Action - REGN
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 18, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/regeneron-pharmaceuticals-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=130419&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between November 2, 2023 and October 30, 2024.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (1) Regeneron paid credit card fees to distributors on the condition that distributors did not charge, one of the Company's primary products, Eylea customers more to use a credit card; (2) these payments subsidized the prices that customers paid when using credit cards to purchase Eylea; (3) as a result, Regeneron offered a price concession that lowered Eylea's selling price; (4) because retina practices were sensitive to higher prices when using credit cards to purchase anti-VEGF medications, Regeneron's price concessions provided a competitive advantage; (5) as a result of the foregoing, Regeneron misleadingly boosted reported Eylea sales; (6) by failing to report its payment of credit card fees as price concessions, Regeneron overstated the ASP reported to federal agencies, thereby violating the False Claims Act; and (7) as a result of the foregoing, defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Regeneron stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/regeneron-pharmaceuticals-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=130419&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. data.title: Zerocap Launches Australia’s First Tailored Crypto Product Linked to CoinDesk 20 Index
data.description:
HONG KONG - Australian digital assets market maker Zerocap has partnered with CoinDesk Indices to offer Australia's first options-based structured products on the CoinDesk 20 Index (CD20), the company said at Consensus Hong Kong.

The partnership introduces sophisticated and tailored investment strategies usually found in traditional markets, a significant advancement for the cryptocurrency industry.

It will allow institutions and other sophisticated market participants to take risk-managed diversified exposure to digital assets that go beyond just bitcoin and ether while offering additional features like downside protection, volatility management and yield enhancement.

The new offering demonstrates the increasing demand for scalable and diversified institutional-grade cryptocurrency products following the debut of spot ETFs in the U.S. last year.

The CoinDesk 20 Index, which has surged 456% over the past five years, provides a diversified alternative to the standard 70/30 bitcoin-ether portfolio split by broadening exposure to other leading crypto assets.

"This partnership with CoinDesk Indices brings sophisticated, structured options to the crypto market for the first time, offering our clients enhanced ways to invest in digital assets with tailored risk and diversification benefits," said Mark Hiriart, head of sales at Zerocap.

Alan Campbell, President of CoinDesk Indices, said the CD 20 Index caters to the growing demand for diversified digital assets exposure and Zerocap's decision to debut structured products tied to the same is a significant step forward in serving global clientele.

data.title: Westpac-Melbourne Institute Leading Index Rises in January; Logs Fastest Pace Since July 2022
data.description:
The Westpac-Melbourne Institute Leading Index, which shows the likely pace of economic activity relative to trend three to nine months into the future, rose to about 0.6% in January from 0.2% in December 2024, according to a Wednesday report.

This is the largest pace of increase since July 2022, around the time of the Reserve Bank of Australia's hiking spree meant to curb rising inflation.

"While the above trend growth pulse is becoming firmer and more convincing, it is still not particularly strong. It remains more consistent with a gradual recovery than a sudden surge," said Tim Robinson, associate professor at Melbourne Institute.

Westpac expects Australian gross domestic product growth to accelerate to 2.2% by year-end from 0.8% over the year to September 2024.

data.title: Maxis's Revenue Could Stay Resilient — Market Talk
data.description: Maxis Bhd.'s revenue could remain resilient as it prioritizes value over price competition, TA Securities analyst Chan Mun Chun says in a note. Growth in the telco's enterprise segment should be supported by ongoing digitalization in both private and public sectors, he says. Maxis has guided for flat-to-low-single-digit Ebitda growth in 2025 and for capital expenditure below MYR1 billion. Chan cuts his 2025-2026 earnings estimates for Maxis by 7.4% and 8.4%, respectively, to factor in the latest guidance and higher depreciation expenses. TA Securities cuts its target price to MYR3.68 from MYR3.80 while maintaining a hold rating on the stock. Shares are 0.3% higher at MYR3.53. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2050 ET - Maxis Bhd.'s revenue could remain resilient as it prioritizes value over price competition, TA Securities analyst Chan Mun Chun says in a note. Growth in the telco's enterprise segment should be supported by ongoing digitalization in both private and public sectors, he says. Maxis has guided for flat-to-low-single-digit Ebitda growth in 2025 and for capital expenditure below MYR1 billion. Chan cuts his 2025-2026 earnings estimates for Maxis by 7.4% and 8.4%, respectively, to factor in the latest guidance and higher depreciation expenses. TA Securities cuts its target price to MYR3.68 from MYR3.80 while maintaining a hold rating on the stock. Shares are 0.3% higher at MYR3.53. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2047 ET - Chinese shares are higher in early trade, led by technology and semiconductor stocks. Investors are awaiting the Two Sessions meeting next week for more details on Beijing's economic growth target this year. Shenzhen Inovance Technology gains 3.7% and Will Semiconductor is up 3.5%. Giga Device Semiconductor rises 2.5%. Among the top decliners, Zhejiang Juhua is down 2.5% and Tianqi Lithium is 1.8% lower. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is up 0.2% at 3330.04, the Shenzhen Composite Index rises 0.6% and the ChiNext Price Index is 0.4% higher. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2043 ET - Hartalega Holdings' long-term outlook may stay positive, supported by the glove maker's ongoing cost rationalization, Public Investment Bank analyst Thye May Ting says in a note. While Chinese manufacturers are expanding into Southeast Asia, she expects minimal near-term impact on Hartalega until FY 2026. While intense competition and global oversupply may pressure glove average selling prices, Hartalega's focus on efficiency and technology should help offset these headwinds, she notes. Thye cuts Hartalega's FY 2025-FY 2026 earnings estimates by 16%-18% after its fiscal 3Q earnings missed expectations. Public IB cuts Hartalega's target price to MYR3.61 from MYR3.65 but keeps an outperform rating. Shares are 2.7% lower at MYR2.54. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2030 ET - Hartalega Holdings' sales volume for January-March could decline by 15% sequentially as U.S. frontloading activities fade, Affin Hwang IB analyst Andrew Lim says in a note. Lim expects the glove maker to register losses in the fiscal 4Q and for its plant utilization rate to drop to 70% in the quarter from 86% in 3Q. Average selling prices may stagnate as China intensifies price competition in non-U.S. markets. Raw material costs could rise slightly as suppliers consolidate capacity, he reckons. Despite the near-term challenges, the company anticipates global glove demand to rise by 10% in 2025, which could support recovery, he notes. Affin Hwang cuts Hartalega's target price to MYR2.80 from MYR3.70 and retains a hold rating. Shares are 1.9% lower at MYR2.56. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2023 ET - While tariffs are the immediate focus of investors in plumbing-fittings supplier Reliance Worldwide, UBS thinks the impact has likely been overestimated. Reliance has around US$120 million of costs exposed to China, so there is some risk, analyst Lee Power says. "But with inventory lags the material financial impact will be FY 2026 at the earliest," UBS says. It notes Reliance's confidence that it's better placed than many rivals. That's because Reliance is the only manufacturer of so-called push to connect fittings in the U.S., says UBS, and this "should largely offset any negative margin implications due to pricing leverage." UBS has a neutral call on Reliance. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2016 ET - Wealth manager Challenger may appear cheap to investors, but Morgan Stanley highlights several headwinds that keep it at equal-weight for now. Analyst Andrei Stadnik points to margins and volumes coming under pressure, and predicts a squeeze on Challenger's dividend given a wide gap between its reported and normalized earnings. "So we think Challenger is likely to trade on lower multiples until positive catalysts emerge," MS says. Its price target falls 9.8% to A$5.95/share. Challenger is up 1.4% at A$5.66 today. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2014 ET - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index edges 0.1% higher to 3930.10, paring opening gains amid possible position adjustments by investors. "While headlines on U.S./Russia/Ukraine peace talks have been a central focus, it seems the noise in markets has been temporarily dialed down a notch," says Chris Weston, head of Research at Pepperstone, in a commentary. Among the best performers on the benchmark index, Genting Singapore rises 2.0%, Sembcorp Industries adds 1.1%, and Jardine Matheson Holdings is up 0.8%. Meanwhile, Wilmar International falls 0.9%, Mapletree Logistics Trust loses 0.8%, and Singapore Exchange is down 0.7%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2008 ET - Uncertainty over Malaysia's 5G dual-network deployment is unlikely to keep weighing on Maxis shares, but the telco still doesn't look ripe for a rerating, Maybank IB analyst Tan Chi Wei says in a note. The stock lacks strong re-rating catalysts over the next 12 months, with Maxis guiding for flat to low-single digit Ebitda growth in 2025 and capital expenditure below MYR1 billion, he notes. Maxis has ventured into the rooftop solar space, but specific details remain limited for now, he adds. Maybank maintains a hold rating on Maxis and keeps the target price at MYR3.70. It prefers Telekom Malaysia among telcos. Shares are 0.6% lower at MYR3.50. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2005 ET - Woodside Energy has suffered from a perfect storm of external factors that have dragged on investor sentiment, says Morgans, which stays bullish on the stock. Woodside's share price is down around 19% over the past 12 months. Morgans says it now trades on a materially lower multiple than rival Santos, despite having higher-margin earnings, a better-quality portfolio of projects and a stronger balance sheet. Analyst Adrian Prendergast retains an add call on Woodside, believing the market has misjudged the company's new Louisiana LNG project "which has a reasonable probability of seeing equity selldowns and solid execution under Woodside/Bechtel management." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2005 ET - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is 0.2% lower at 1581.84. The market is expected to trade within 1580-1590 as investors assess earnings and await fresh catalysts, Apex Securities says in a note. Sentiment may remain fragile due to geopolitical tensions and foreign fund outflows, the brokerage reckons, adding that caution is advised until clearer signals emerge. It favors defensive plays like plantation and REITs, while expecting export-oriented sectors to benefit from a weaker ringgit, boosting trade activity and competitiveness. Among decliners, Maxis Bhd. is 1.4% lower and Kuala Lumpur Kepong is down 1.6%. Meanwhile, CelcomDigi adds 0.6% and Axiata Group is up 0.5%.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1958 ET - Japanese auto stocks are lower after President Trump said he was considering tariffs of 25% or more on automobiles. The auto tariffs could negatively affect sales of Japanese cars or their profitability in the U.S. market. Toyota Motor, for instance, sold 2.3 million cars in the U.S. last year. Of that, about 23% were exported from Japan. The administration has said that action is likely after April 1. Trump said he would allow "a little bit of a chance" to re-shore production, without providing details. Toyota Motor is down 0.8%, Honda Motor is 0.9% lower and Subaru Corp. is down 1.9%. The benchmark Nikkei Stock Average is flat. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

1938 ET - ARB Corp.'s bull at Citi is feeling more confident in the Australian auto-accessory retailer's U.S. strategy. Analyst Sam Teeger acknowledges gross margins are under pressure from the weaker Aussie dollar but points to the emerging profitability of ARB's part-owned 4 Wheel Parts business. Teeger, who hadn't expected breakeven until fiscal 2027, says this shows strong execution and highlights a 25% increase in ARB product sales by 4WP since its acquisition. Citi lifts its target price 4.0% to A$51.20 and keeps a buy rating on the stock, which is down 0.3% at A$39.76. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Bank of Korea Set to Trim 2025 Growth Forecast, Resume Cuts — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of Korea is set to trim its 2025 gross domestic product growth forecast next week and resume rate cuts, Barclays economist Bum Ki Son writes in a note. Son expects the central bank to revise this year's GDP estimate down to 1.5% from its earlier projections of 1.9% in November and a 1.6%-1.7% range last month. The anticipated downgrade is likely a reflection of uncertainty and potential shocks over U.S. tariff plans, in addition to political instability over President Yoon's impeachment, he says. He expects the BOK to cut its policy rate by 25 bps to 2.75% at the meeting on Tuesday next week, as forex volatility eases while economic fundamentals worsen. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: China New Home Prices Fall the Most in 6 Months
data.description:
China’s new home prices in 70 cities declined by 5.0% year-on-year in January 2025, easing from December’s 5.3% drop and marking the smallest decline since last July.

Beijing’s ongoing efforts to curb the prolonged property downturn by lowering mortgage rates, reducing homebuying costs, and allowing local governments to purchase unsold housing and idle land using special bond proceeds.

However, the latest result marked the 19th consecutive month of decreases

data.title: Oil gains on U.S., Russia supply curtailments as market seeks Ukraine talks clarity
data.description:
Oil prices edged higher on Wednesday amid oil supply disruptions in the U.S. and Russia and as markets awaited clarity on the Ukraine peace talks.

Brent crude futures gained 20 cents, or 0.3% at $76.04 a barrel at 0146 GMT, climbing for a third day.

U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures for March rose 23 cents, or 0.3%, to $72.08 a barrel, up 1.7% from the close on Friday after not settling on Monday because of the Presidents' Day public holiday. The March contract expires on Thursday and the more active April contract gained 0.3% to $72.04.

Russia said oil flows through the Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC), a major route for crude exports from Kazakhstan, were reduced by 30%-40% on Tuesday after a Ukrainian drone attack on a pumping station. A 30% cut would equate to the loss of 380,000 barrels per day of supply to the market, according to Reuters calculations.

Meanwhile, cold weather threatened U.S. oil supply, with the North Dakota Pipeline Authority estimating that production in the country's No. 3 producing state would be down by as much as 150,000 bpd because of the cold.

U.S. President Donald Trump's administration said on Tuesday it had agreed to hold more talks with Russia on ending the war in Ukraine. A deal could ease or help remove sanctions that have disrupted the flows of Russian oil shipments.

Israel and Hamas will also begin indirect negotiations on a second stage of the Gaza ceasefire deal, officials said on Tuesday.

However, Trump said on Tuesday he intends to impose auto tariffs "in the neighbourhood of 25%" and similar duties on semiconductors and pharmaceutical imports. Tariffs could raise prices for consumer products, weaken the economy and reduce demand for fuel.

data.title: Hartalega's Long-Term Outlook May Remain Positive — Market Talk
data.description: Hartalega Holdings' long-term outlook may stay positive, supported by the glove maker's ongoing cost rationalization, Public Investment Bank analyst Thye May Ting says in a note. While Chinese manufacturers are expanding into Southeast Asia, she expects minimal near-term impact on Hartalega until FY 2026. While intense competition and global oversupply may pressure glove average selling prices, Hartalega's focus on efficiency and technology should help offset these headwinds, she notes. Thye cuts Hartalega's FY 2025-FY 2026 earnings estimates by 16%-18% after its fiscal 3Q earnings missed expectations. Public IB cuts Hartalega's target price to MYR3.61 from MYR3.65 but keeps an outperform rating. Shares are 2.7% lower at MYR2.54. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Samsung names semiconductor experts as new directors
data.description: South Korean tech giant Samsung Electronics Co. has named several semiconductor experts, including Jun Young-hyun, vice chairman and head of the Device Solutions (DS) division, as new registered directors. The move comes as the company sees the importance of allocating semiconductor experts at the highest decision-making level, given that the semiconductor industry is growing significantly in the era of artificial intelligence (AI). Samsung Electronics announced on Tuesday that it will submit a proposal for director appointments at the upcoming regular general shareholders’ meeting scheduled for March 19th, 2025, at the Suwon Convention Center in Gyeonggi Province. The company nominated Jun, the head of the DS division, and Song Jai-hyuk, the chief technology officer (CTO) of the DS division and president of the division’s Semiconductor R&D Center, as new internal directors. It also nominated Lee Hyuk-jae, a chip expert and professor of electrical and computer engineering at Seoul National University, as a new external director. Lee graduated from Seoul National University’s Department of Electronics Engineering and obtained a Ph.D. in engineering from Purdue University in the United States. He served as an assistant professor at Louisiana Tech University and a senior engineer at Intel Corp. before joining Seoul National University as a professor in 2001. He also served as the president of the Institute of Electronics and Information Engineers (IEIE). He currently holds multiple positions, including director of the System Semiconductor Industry Promotion Center at Seoul National University, co-chair of the Semiconductor Special Committee at the National Academy of Engineering of Korea, and director of the Semiconductor Joint Research Institute at Seoul National University. With these appointments, the company’s board of directors will have three semiconductor experts, marking the first time in three years that the board has had three or more semiconductor specialists. Several current board members are also up for reappointment, including Roh Tae-moon, president and head of the Mobile eXperience (MX) Business division, Kim Jun-sung, chief investment officer (CIO) of the National University of Singapore Endowment Fund, Heo Eun-nyeong, professor of engineering at the Seoul National University, and Yoo Myung-hee, former head of trade negotiations at the Ministry of Trade, Industry, and Energy. For the audit committee, the company plans to propose the appointments of Yoo and Shin Je-yoon, former chairman of the Financial Services Commission (FSC). The chairman of the board will be elected at the newly formed board meeting following the general shareholders’ meeting, with Shin emerging as a strong candidate for the position. The proposal to appoint the company’s Executive Chairman Jay Y. Lee as an internal director has been excluded from the agenda. The company is known to have internally assessed that Lee‘s legal risks have not been entirely eliminated due to the prosecution’s appeal to the Supreme Court regarding allegations of unfair mergers and accounting fraud. Lee is the only one serving as a non-registered executive among the heads of Korea’s four largest conglomerates. “Given the various obstacles, including investor relations and legal risks, it seems the company is carefully considering the matter,” Samsung Group’s Compliance Committee Chairman Lee Chan-hee told reporters on Tuesday. “However, I believe Chairman Lee should return as a registered director.” Regarding the delayed discussions on restoring the group’s control tower, he said, “There are so many different perspectives that there is no unified opinion even within the compliance committee. However, I personally believe a control tower is necessary. The company will carefully consider how to create and operate it.”

data.title: Korea’s wealthy investors flock to U.S. long-term treasury bonds
data.description: South Korea’s wealthiest investors have heavily invested in U.S. Treasury securities, particularly long-term bonds. Net purchases by individual investors surged to a total of 1 trillion won ($693 million) in January 2025 alone. Their total investment in U.S. Treasury securities during the first month of 2025 was more than three times higher than the same month during the previous year. According to data from Samsung Securities Co., among the 2,250 deep-pocketed investors who purchased U.S. government bonds in January 2025, nearly 33 percent invested between 100 million and 1 billion won. Notably, 75 percent of the company’s bond sales were concentrated in 30-year Treasury securities, with more than 360 billion won to bonds maturing in May 2050. Financial experts suggest that now is a favorable time to invest in long-term U.S. bonds, as inflation in the United States hovers around 3 percent and interest rate cuts remain uncertain. Bond prices are currently at some of their lowest levels thanks to the U.S. Federal Reserve’s modest rate cuts. U.S. long-term bonds are seen as a strategic hedge against U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariff policies. The U.S. government is also using tariff exemptions for its allies as an incentive to encourage investments in ultra-long-term Treasury bonds.

data.title: EcoPro CEOs to receive 30% of salaries in shares amid $218 bn loss
data.description: The CEOs of EcoPro Group’s four listed companies will receive 30 percent of their annual salaries in company stock, demonstrating their commitment to responsible management and shareholder value enhancement. EcoPro announced on Tuesday that the new compensation policy will apply to the CEOs of EcoPro, EcoPro BM, EcoPro HN, and EcoPro Materials, its four listed affiliates. Under this policy, CEO Song Ho-jun (EcoPro), Choi Moon-ho (EcoPro BM), Kim Jong-seop (EcoPro HN), and Kim Byung-hoon (EcoPro Materials) will receive only 70 percent of their salaries in cash, with the remaining 30 percent paid in company shares at the end of the year. An EcoPro spokesperson said that the move reflects the CEOs’ determination to restore profitability and enhance shareholder value. The decision follows a challenging year for EcoPro, as slowing demand for electric vehicles (EVs) led to significant financial losses. The company posted an operating loss of 314.5 billion won ($218 million) in 2024, a sharp reversal from its 298.2 billion won profit in 2023. Revenue also plummeted by 57.2 percent year-over-year to 3.11 trillion won. During recent management meetings, the CEOs acknowledged their responsibility for the company’s declining performance and falling stock prices. They collectively agreed to prioritize a return to profitability in 2025, symbolizing their commitment by voluntarily accepting a portion of their salaries in stock. EcoPro’s Song said that achieving a turnaround in 2025 is a top priority to meet market expectations. EcoPro expects profitability to improve in the first quarter, driven by a recovery in key industries. During a recent earnings conference call, EcoPro BM Vice President Kim Jang-woo projected that annual sales volumes could increase by around 40 percent, citing inventory reductions at major customers and new vehicle launches. He also noted that cost-cutting measures and reduced fixed expenses would contribute to a significant improvement in operating profit. EcoPro has a history of sharing its growth with employees, a principle established by founder Lee Dong-chae. The company’s board approved Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) for all employees in October 2022, with shares allocated based on position, tenure, and salary. Employees received RSUs worth 15 to 20 percent of their salaries, with 146,505 shares distributed in October 2024. The remaining shares are set to be issued in October 2025.

data.title: China Home Prices Show Slight Improvement But Remain in Decline
data.description:
Home prices in 70 major Chinese cities continued to slightly narrow their declines in January, suggesting that Beijing's policy blitz has helped stabilize the beleaguered real-estate sector.

Average home prices in the 70 cities surveyed by the government dropped 0.07% month-on-month in January versus the 0.08% decline recorded in December, according to calculations by The Wall Street Journal based on data released Wednesday by the National Bureau of Statistics.

There were 42 cities reporting month-on-month home price falls last month, down from 43 in December.

Compared with a year ago, average home prices in the major cities dropped 5.4% in January, narrowing from the 5.7% decrease recorded in December. Of the 70 cities, 68 reported year-on-year price declines last month, the same as in December.

Write to Singapore editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Celltrion secures EU approval for 2 osteoporosis biosimilar drugs
data.description: Celltrion announced on Tuesday that it received European Commission (EC) approval for Stoboclo and Osenvelt, its biosimilars referencing Prolia and Xgeva (denosumab). Prolia and Xgeva share the same active ingredient, with Prolia approved for osteoporosis treatment and Xgeva for preventing skeletal-related complications in cancer patients with bone metastases. Stoboclo has been approved for postmenopausal osteoporosis and bone loss treatment, while Osenvelt is indicated for preventing bone metastasis complications in cancer patients and treating giant cell tumors of the bone, mirroring the indications of their reference biologics. Both biosimilars were also approved in South Korea in November 2024 and recently completed regulatory filings in the United States, all indicative of the company’s smooth progress in key global markets. The combined global sales of Prolia and Xgeva totaled around 9 trillion won ($6.24 billion) in 2024. With its latest EU approval, Celltrion aims to accelerate market expansion domestically and internationally. This is Celltrion’s third major regulatory approval in February 2025, following the previous week’s EC approval of Eydenzelt, the company’s biosimilar for Eylea.

data.title: India’s banks will struggle to keep equities crown
data.description:
India’s dealmakers are celebrating their arrival on the global map. Last year, Kotak Mahindra Bank not only topped LSEG's league table for initial public offerings in Asia by volume, edging out CITIC and JPMorgan , but it also broke into the ranks of the top 10 underwriters of common stock deals globally by proceeds. Both are firsts for an Indian investment bank. But the strong showing by the $45 billion firm and its compatriots may prove hard to sustain.

A record $71 billion in equity fundraising powered the South Asian country's climb past China and Hong Kong to the spot of the world’s second-largest destination for share placements behind the U.S. last year, per Dealogic data. New-economy companies including Swiggy and Ola Electric Mobility going public were a lynchpin for strong fees. Meanwhile, punchy valuations prompted global businesses like Whirlpool to cash out stakes in their local units and Hyundai Motor to take its Indian business public.

It spelt a bonanza for banks like Kotak and ICICI Bank , both of which trade at 3 times forward book value, the top of their peer group. Their rise up the league tables buys them credibility beyond those rich valuations.

The mood is upbeat. At a Mumbai conference of investment banks in January, a singer belted out chest-thumping patriotic numbers in the presence of Madhabi Puri Buch, chief of Securities and Exchange Board of India, the capital markets regulator. Sundararaman Ramamurthy, the CEO of BSE , one of the country’s two main stock exchanges, described the IPO boom as a moment of India’s “re-emergence” on the world stage.

The pipeline remains strong. Kotak has won a mandate, alongside Morgan Stanley , for what could be India's largest ever IPO, an up to $4.6 billion listing of Reliance Industries' telecommunications business, IFR reported in January, citing unnamed people. HDFC Bank’s shadow lending unit has filed for a $1.44 billion float. Businesses ranging from the local unit of South Korean consumer appliances giant LG Electronics to Tiger Global-backed stockbroker Groww are preparing for billion-dollar listings too, per IFR. Kotak expects primary fundraising in India to rise 59% from last year’s level to $35 billion in 2025.

But the broader environment is less cheery. Foreign portfolio investors are dumping Indian shares and companies are reporting dismal earnings, pulling indexes off last year’s dizzying highs. The outlook for GDP growth is sombre. Beijing's push for higher-valued startups could rejuvenate dealmaking in China this year, and Hong Kong listings are rebounding from a 20-year low. The two centres notched up a total $132 billion in equity transactions in 2023 before markets slumped.

Kotak and its peers may find their dealmaking crown was easier to earn than to hold.

Follow @ShritamaBose on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Kotak Mahindra Bank was the 10th largest bookrunner globally for common stock deals by proceeds in 2024, with a 1.5% share of the market, according to LSEG data. It also topped the league table for Asian initial public offerings, including Chinese A-shares, facilitating listings that raised $2 billion during the year.
